## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do? Keep it concise. -->

-

## Type

<!-- The PR title MUST follow Conventional Commits: type(scope): description -->
<!-- Examples: feat(ui): add carousel, fix(network): handle timeout, chore: update deps -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `refactor` — code restructure (no behavior change)
- [ ] `chore` — maintenance (deps, CI, docs)
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — test additions or fixes

## Validation

- [ ] `make lint` passes
- [ ] `make build` passes
- [ ] `make test` passes

## API Impact

- [ ] No public API changes
- [ ] Public API changed — reviewed intentionally

## GitFlow

<!-- Which flow does this PR follow? -->

- [ ] `feature/*` → `develop`
- [ ] `release/*` → `main`
- [ ] `hotfix/*` → `main`
- [ ] `develop` → `main` (direct release)
